title: 18" Kitchen Base Cabinet (Face Frame) — Trash Pull-Out or Storage
summary: A face-frame 18"-wide kitchen base cabinet built to standard dimensions, set up for a trash pull-out or a door cupboard. Real cabinetmaking from a half sheet of plywood.
description:
This is a proper 18"-wide kitchen base cabinet — face-frame construction, a 2x4 toe-kick base, plywood box, and your choice of a trash pull-out or a hinged storage door. It's built to standard cabinet dimensions so it can slot into a run of kitchen cabinets, and it's genuine cabinetmaking: pocket-hole joinery, a clamped-and-squared face frame, and drawer-slide hardware.

The plan is built around 3/4" plywood ripped into 22-3/4" strips (called '1x24' throughout) — you buy a full sheet but only need half, saving the center scrap for the supports. A workflow tip that saves real frustration: drill all your pocket holes before assembling anything, because drilling pocket holes into a mounted trash-can opening later is miserable.

The face frame is the part that makes it look like a kitchen cabinet: build it flat on a table with clamps, square, joined with 3/4" pocket holes, then attach to the box front with a 1/4" side overhang. Everything after that depends on your hardware and your trash can. Side-mount slides usually need a 1/2" plywood spacer to bring them flush with the inside of the face frame; mount them at the height of your can. The pull-out is set up for two cans side by side but adapts easily — more compartments, or a bottom-mounted pull-out platform (add cross supports for strength if you go that way). The trash pull-out attaches to the door, flush at the bottom with a 1/4" gap each side. Add the back last (after the slides are in, so you can reach inside), and buy or build a 30-1/4" x 17-1/2" door to suit.

tools.note: Box and face frame joinery — drill all holes before assembly.
Ripping the sheet into accurate 22-3/4" strips.
Clamp the face frame square while assembling.

materials.name: Hardwood plywood, 3/4 in, 4x8 sheet
materials.note: Ripped into 22-3/4" strips; half a sheet used, scrap kept for supports.

materials.name: Plywood, 1/2 in (slide spacers)
materials.note: Only if side-mount slides need it.

steps.title: Drill all pocket holes, then attach the bottom
steps.body: Drill all your pocket holes before assembling anything — it's far easier on flat boards than around a mounted trash bin later. Attach the bottom to the sides with glue and 1-1/4" pocket screws.

steps.title: Add the back support
steps.body: Add the back support, using the scraps from cutting the plywood or a 1x4/1x3. This ties the back of the box together.

steps.title: Build and attach the face frame
steps.body: Build the face frame flat on a smooth table with clamps, square, joining with 3/4" pocket holes, 1-1/4" screws, and glue. Mark the sides at 1/4" for the side overhang and attach the frame to the cabinet face through predrilled pocket holes.

steps.title: Add the countertop supports and base
steps.body: Attach the countertop supports (also fasten them to the sides, not just the front, for lateral support). Build the 2x4 toe-kick base and attach it under the cabinet.

steps.title: Mount the slides
steps.body: Most remaining steps depend on your slide hardware. For standard side-mount slides, add a 1/2" plywood spacer to bring the slides flush with the inside of the face frame. Mount them at the height of your trash can.

steps.title: Build the trash pull-out
steps.body: Build the pull-out from 1x4 to hold your can(s) — this plan fits two side by side, but you can add compartments or put the pull-out on the bottom as a platform. If you make it a bottom platform, add cross supports for strength. Size it to your actual can.

steps.title: Attach the pull-out to the door
steps.body: Attach the trash pull-out assembly to the door with screws, keeping the door flush at the bottom and with a 1/4" gap on each side so it clears the face frame.

steps.title: Add the back and finish
steps.body: Add the back last — after the slides are installed, so you could reach inside to work. Use glue and 1-1/4" finish nails. Sand and finish or paint to match your kitchen.

images.alt: 18-inch face-frame kitchen base cabinet with a trash pull-out
